name: Camino de Las Haciendas — return from Trebujena
name_original: Camino de Las Haciendas — vuelta desde Trebujena
region_intro: Hiking around Trebujena offers diverse landscapes, from expansive marshlands influenced by the Guadalquivir River to rolling hills covered in vineyards. The region borders the Doñana Natural Park, providing a significant wetland ecosystem known for birdwatching. The terrain includes gentle slopes of white earth, contrasting with the flat marshlands. These natural features define the hiking experience in Trebujena. Best hiking trails around Trebujena The most popular hiking route is Camino de Las Haciendas — return from Trebujena, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This path winds through vineyards and offers views from Mirador del Redondo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salinas and Marismas de Trebujena Trail, a moderate 10.0 miles (16.0 km) path. This route connects to the Doñana Natural Park boundary and is known for birdwatching opportunities. Local hikers also love the Pinar de la Algaida loop from Pinar de la Algaida, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through pine forest, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Trebujena is defined by marshlands, vineyards, and river views. The network offers options for different ability levels, with more than 20 hiking trails available. The routes in Trebujena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Trebujena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails in Trebujena?
A: Hiking in Trebujena offers a diverse landscape. You'll find trails winding through expansive &lt;strong&gt;marshlands&lt;/strong&gt; influenced by the Guadalquivir River, which are excellent for birdwatching. Other routes traverse rolling hills covered in picturesque &lt;strong&gt;vineyards&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by gentle slopes of white earth. The region's proximity to the Doñana Natural Park means many trails offer views and access to significant wetland ecosystems.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Trebujena?
A: Yes, Trebujena offers several easy hiking options suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31567443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Pinar de la Algaida&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9 km trail that takes just under an hour to complete, making it ideal for shorter outings with children. There are 9 easy routes in total, providing gentle paths for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Trebujena?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Trebujena, especially those in natural areas like the marshlands and vineyards. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife, particularly in areas bordering the Doñana Natural Park, which is a crucial habitat for many bird species. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Trebujena?
A: Yes, Trebujena features several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009035470/camino-de-las-haciendas-return-from-trebujena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Las Haciendas — return from Trebujena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.5 km path winding through vineyards. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10295816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinar de la Algaida loop from Pinar de la Algaida&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.3 km trail through a pine forest.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while hiking in Trebujena?
A: While hiking in Trebujena, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Marshes&lt;/a&gt; are a significant natural feature, offering prime birdwatching opportunities. Along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009039266/salinas-and-marismas-de-trebujena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Marismas de Trebujena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can see the historic 18th-century &lt;em&gt;Cortijo de Alventus&lt;/em&gt; and the &lt;em&gt;Guadalquivir pier&lt;/em&gt;. The &lt;em&gt;Mirador del Redondo&lt;/em&gt;, found on the Camino de Las Haciendas, provides expansive views over the vineyards and the Guadalquivir River. You might also spot the remains of the &lt;em&gt;Trebujena Windmill&lt;/em&gt;, offering panoramic views over the village.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Trebujena?
A: The best time to hike in Trebujena is typically during the cooler months, from &lt;strong&gt;autumn to spring&lt;/strong&gt; (October to May). During these periods, the temperatures are more pleasant for outdoor activities. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers comfortable weather and stunning sunsets over the marshes. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late evening hikes more advisable.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Trebujena is an exceptional destination for birdwatching, especially due to its extensive marshlands bordering the Doñana Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009039266/salinas-and-marismas-de-trebujena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Marismas de Trebujena Trail&lt;/a&gt; is particularly renowned for its high concentration of diverse bird species, including endangered ones like the marbled teal and white-headed ducks. The &lt;em&gt;Seno de Esparraguera&lt;/em&gt; along this route is a particularly rich area for birdlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Trebujena?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Trebujena, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique blend of landscapes, from the serene marshlands to the picturesque vineyards, and the abundant opportunities for birdwatching. The well-maintained paths and the stunning views, especially at sunset over the Guadalquivir, are frequently highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Trebujena?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails available around Trebujena, offering a variety of options for different abilities and interests. This includes 9 easy routes and 19 moderate routes, ensuring there's a path for everyone to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that connect to Doñana Natural Park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009039266/salinas-and-marismas-de-trebujena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Marismas de Trebujena Trail&lt;/a&gt; directly connects Trebujena's urban center with the boundary of the Doñana Natural Park. This trail offers an excellent opportunity to experience the park's buffer zone and its rich wetland ecosystem, known for its diverse birdlife.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Guadalquivir River?
A: Many trails in Trebujena offer scenic views of the Guadalquivir River, which significantly influences the local landscape. The &lt;em&gt;Guadalquivir pier&lt;/em&gt;, accessible via trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009039266/salinas-and-marismas-de-trebujena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Marismas de Trebujena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, provides excellent vantage points. Additionally, the &lt;em&gt;Mirador del Redondo&lt;/em&gt; on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009035470/camino-de-las-haciendas-return-from-trebujena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Las Haciendas&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views that include the river.

Q: Are there any trails specifically through vineyards?
A: Yes, Trebujena is known for its viticulture, and you can hike directly through vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009035470/camino-de-las-haciendas-return-from-trebujena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Las Haciendas — return from Trebujena&lt;/a&gt; is a unique circular path in Cádiz that winds through these picturesque vineyards, offering a beautiful backdrop for your hike.
tips: Route that runs between vineyards and this trail covers different places with the main point of interest being the redondón viewpoint, where you can see the characteristic landscape of Trebujena joining the town with the Guadalquivir River.

Ruins of what was one of the largest oil mills in the area. It is located outside the urban area about 2 km in what is called "road of the haciendas".
At present, a large tower of about 10 meters high is preserved, which makes it visible from many points.

Trebujena is a town located on the administrative border between Cádiz and Seville, in an area that descends to one of the thickest meanders of the Río Guadalquivir. The Plaza España houses the town hall and the Purísima Concepción Parish.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Molino de las Haciendas
